description:
type_fn's fixpoint loop iterates f.blocks in storage order (line 1159: 'for b in &f.blocks'). For functions with back-edges (loops), this means the loop body is visited before the back-edge predecessor has been processed, requiring extra outer iterations to converge.

Add: fn topo_order(f: &FnIr) -> Vec<BlockId>
  BFS from f.entry. Track visited set. Edges that would revisit a node are back-edges — record them but don't enqueue. Return BFS order (guaranteed to visit each predecessor before its successors for all non-back edges).

Replace 'for b in &f.blocks' with 'for &bid in &topo' in the main propagation loop. The outer 'loop { … if !changed { break } }' still handles back-edges — they just require fewer iterations because forward edges converge in one pass.

For DAG-shaped functions (continuations, most lambdas — the majority of CPS output), the fixpoint terminates after a single forward pass with no second iteration needed.

No change to the merge_into / narrow_for_if logic. Test: a fixture with a loop; verify the inferred types are identical to before. Verify a DAG-only function converges in one iteration (add an iteration counter to type_fn gated on cfg(test)).
title: replace storage-order block traversal in type_fn with topological order
